quiz1.title: What does the expression 10 / 2 evaluate to?

help.concept: Division operator (/) divides the left operand by the right operand.
help.hint_2: Remember that division gives you the quotient.

quiz2.title: Which operator checks if two values are equal?
quiz2.hint: This operator returns True if both values are the same.

help.concept: The equality operator checks if two values are equal.
help.hint_2: Think about how you would ask if two things are the same.

sketch1.title: Understanding Arithmetic Operators
sketch1.bodyMarkdown:
Arithmetic operators are used to perform mathematical operations. The most common arithmetic operators are addition (+), subtraction (-), multiplication (*), and division (/). For example, if you want to add two numbers, you can write:

```python
result = 5 + 3
print(result)  # This will output 8
```

Try it yourself: Change the numbers and see what the output is!

Line by line: read each statement from top to bottom. Notice which names are created, which values are passed into functions, and what output the code is meant to show.

sketch2.title: Comparison Operators
sketch2.bodyMarkdown:
Comparison operators compare two values and return a boolean result (True or False). The common comparison operators are equal (==), not equal (!=), greater than (>), and less than (<). For example:

```python
is_equal = (5 == 5)
print(is_equal)  # This will output True
```

Your turn: Try comparing different values!

Line by line: read each statement from top to bottom. Notice which names are created, which values are passed into functions, and what output the code is meant to show.

sketch3.title: Boolean Operators
sketch3.bodyMarkdown:
Boolean operators are used to combine conditional statements. The main boolean operators are AND, OR, and NOT. For example:

```python
is_true = (5 > 3) and (3 < 10)
print(is_true)  # This will output True
```

Try this: Change the conditions and see how the output changes!

Line by line: read each statement from top to bottom. Notice which names are created, which values are passed into functions, and what output the code is meant to show.
